# Chapter 6: Time, frequency, and signals  (~80 pp)

- 6.1 Time as the most precisely measured quantity (10)
- 6.2 Clocks: pendulum, quartz, atomic, optical (12)
- 6.3 Frequency, period, phase (10)
- 6.4 Sampling and aliasing (Nyquist informally) (16)
- 6.5 Time-frequency duality preview (12)
- 6.6 GPS as a measurement system (12)
- 6.7 Failure section: clock drift and the Patriot missile (8)

**Archetype**: scaling (across thirty orders of magnitude in time), interface.
**Project**: measure your home's mains frequency over a 24-hour period; compare to the grid operator's published data.
**Exercises**: 28.
